info.title: Student Project Showcase Portal API
info.description: API documentation for the Student Project Showcase Portal backend. Provides endpoints for User Authentication, Project Creation/Management, User following, Project liking, and Event-driven Notifications.

get.description: Tests the connection to the database by executing a simple query.

200.description: Database is connected and application is healthy.

500.description: Database is disconnected or another server error occurred.

post.description: Simulates authentication during local development. Finds or creates a user with the specified email and returns a signed JWT. This endpoint is disabled in production.

email.description: Unique email address for the user.

name.description: Display name. If not provided, it defaults to the username part of the email.

200.description: Mock login successful. Returns the JWT token and user info.

404.description: Mock login is not available in production.

error.example: Mock login is not available in production.

get.description: Redirects the client browser to the Google OAuth consent screen to authenticate using a Google account.
Endpoint called by Google after successful authentication. Issues an HttpOnly token cookie and redirects the user back to the frontend.

302.description: Redirects to frontend homepage on success, or to `/login?error=auth_failed` on failure. Sets the `token` cookie.

get.description: Retrieves the user profile details for the currently authenticated session.

401.description: Unauthorized. Missing, invalid, or expired session token.

post.description: Invalidates the current session by clearing the authentication cookie.

get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of all student projects. Can filter by student ID.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of items per page (defaults to 10).

parameters.name: studentId
parameters.description: Filter projects by a specific student UUID.

post.description: Creates a new project showcasing a student's work. Only students can create projects. File uploads are supported for the thumbnail image.

description.description: Detailed explanation of the project features, architecture, and technology.
description.example: Built with Node.js, Express, Docker, and PostgreSQL. Features robust event communication.

repositoryUrl.description: Link to the GitHub/GitLab repository. Must start with http:// or https://.

thumbnail.description: Image file for the project thumbnail (jpeg, jpg, png, or gif. Max 5MB).

400.description: Validation failed or file upload error.

403.description: Forbidden. Only users with the STUDENT role can perform this action.

get.description: Retrieves the detail of a single project by its unique UUID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique UUID of the project.

put.description: Updates an existing project. Only the student who owns the project can update it.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique UUID of the project.

description.example: Updated configuration to support Kubernetes orchestration and unified logging.

400.description: Validation failed, invalid UUID, or file error.

403.description: Forbidden. You can only update your own projects.

delete.description: Deletes a project from the catalog. Only the student who owns the project or an administrator can delete it. This deletes the associated DB records and cleans up the thumbnail file from the disk.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique UUID of the project.

403.description: Forbidden. You do not have permission to delete this project.

post.description: Allows an authenticated user to like a project. Triggers a PROJECT_LIKED event, generating a notification for the project owner.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique UUID of the project.

400.description: Invalid request parameters or already liked.

delete.description: Allows an authenticated user to remove their like from a project.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique UUID of the project.

404.description: Like relation not found (user had not liked this project).

post.description: Allows the current authenticated user to follow another user (typically a recruiter following a student). Triggers a NEW_FOLLOWER event, generating a notification for the followed user.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique UUID of the user to follow.

400.description: Cannot follow yourself, already following, or invalid UUID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique UUID of the user to unfollow.

404.description: Follow relationship not found (not following this user).

get.description: Retrieves the current authenticated user's notifications, ordered by creation date (newest first), with pagination support.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of notifications per page (defaults to 10).

patch.description: Marks all unread notifications belonging to the currently authenticated user as read.
Marks a specific notification, identified by its unique UUID, as read.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique UUID of the notification.

200.description: Notification updated successfully. Returns the updated object.

403.description: Forbidden. You can only mark your own notifications as read.

put.description: Allows the currently authenticated user to update their display name and upload a profile picture.

get.description: Retrieves the list of student creators followed by the currently authenticated recruiter.

200.description: List of followed users fetched successfully.

get.description: Retrieves details of all users registered on the platform, including project and follower counts. Restricted to users with the ADMIN role.
Retrieves the account information for a user by their unique UUID. Restricted to users with the ADMIN role.

put.description: Modifies the profile name, email, or role permissions of a specific user. Restricted to users with the ADMIN role.

400.description: Email already in use or validation error.

delete.description: Deletes a user account and cascading data (like published projects and follow relationships). Restricted to ADMIN role. Cannot delete own logged-in session.

BearerAuth.description: Provide a JWT bearer token in the Authorization header. Typically used during development mock login.

CookieAuth.name: token
CookieAuth.description: HttpOnly cookie named `token` containing the session JWT. Automatically set on Google OAuth callback.

description.example: Built with Node.js, Express, Docker, and PostgreSQL. Features robust event communication.

actorId.description: User ID who triggered the action.

entityId.description: UUID of the related entity (e.g. project ID or follower ID).

message.example: Title must be at least 3 characters long
